disclaimer: This feed reports that an official source page changed, with the passage that changed. It does not assert what the law is, and it is not legal advice. Every item was reviewed by a named human before publication. An empty `changes` array means no human has confirmed a change yet — it is NOT a claim that nothing changed at any watched source.

registry_verification.statement: THE SOURCE REGISTRY IS NOT HUMAN-VERIFIED. Each entry is a CANDIDATE official URL: our own crawler fetched it and read its title, and its `verification_status` says whether a named human has since confirmed it is the official page for that document class in that jurisdiction. `unverified` means nobody has. A machine cannot tell a state's real birth-certificate page from a convincing-looking one — it cannot even tell a live page from a bot-wall served with HTTP 200 — so do not rely on an unverified entry as authoritative guidance, and do not present it to anyone as one. What this tool does claim: this URL changed, and here is what changed in it. What it never claims: what the law is.

sources.authority: Arizona Department of Health Services, Bureau of Vital Records
sources.verification_statement: UNVERIFIED — machine-checked, not human-confirmed. A live fetch confirmed this URL answers and its title was read; NO HUMAN has confirmed that it is the official page for this document class in this jurisdiction. Do not rely on it as authoritative guidance.
sources.notes: live-fetched. HTTP 200. The <title> is the unhelpful site-wide 'ADHS', so the page was confirmed on its body text, which normalizes to ~5.6k characters of Vital Records content — a reminder that a title alone is not a verification.

sources.authority: Arizona Department of Transportation, Motor Vehicle Division
sources.verification_statement: UNVERIFIED — machine-checked, not human-confirmed. A live fetch confirmed this URL answers and its title was read; NO HUMAN has confirmed that it is the official page for this document class in this jurisdiction. Do not rely on it as authoritative guidance.
sources.notes: live-fetched. HTTP 200, title 'Driver Services | Department of Transportation'. NOT the obvious URL, and that is the point: azdot.gov/mvd — the MVD landing page — was in this registry for one day and was caught by the first real two-run watch pass as a FALSE-DRIFT source. It renders a 'frequently viewed links' widget populated with a random sample of site links, which re-rolls on a slow cycle, so the page hashed differently between two watch runs five minutes apart with a diff consisting entirely of link titles ('rest area rules' → 'penalties'). It would have minted a change record most weeks, forever. This deeper page carries the same driver-services content and no rotating widget.
